name: Imam Bayaldi ( Eggplant Stuffed with Aromatic
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the eggplants and cut off the stem end if using large ones. Cut in half lengthwise.
2. Make at least 3 lengthwise slashes on the cut sides of the eggplants, being careful not to pierce the skin on the opposite side. Sprinkle with salt and let stand for 30 minutes.
3. Rinse with cold water, dry, and invert to drain.
4. In a small pan, simmer onions with 1/2 cup water for a few minutes. Drain and discard the water or save for soup.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a medium frying pan and saute onions until soft.
6. Put approximately a third of the onions in the bottom of a buttered casserole dish large enough to accommodate all the eggplants.
7. Add 4-5 tomato slices over the onions in the casserole and add the rest of the tomatoes to the onion remaining in the frying pan.
8. Saute onions and tomatoes for 10 minutes, then stir in all but 2 tablespoons of parsley.
9. Remove from heat and set eggplants into the casserole, tucking a slice of garlic into each eggplant slash.
10. Stuff the slashes with the filling, allowing some to cover the top of the eggplant.
11. Season lightly with salt, pepper, and a pinch of sugar.
12. Dribble the remaining oil and chopped parsley over the eggplants.
13. Cover the casserole with a lid or aluminum foil and bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 30-40 minutes, until fork-tender.
14. Remove cover during last 10 minutes to allow sauce to thicken.
15. Garnish with parsley and serve warm.
Note: This dish is excellent as a first course of a subsequently light meal topped with fresh fruit and Turkish coffee. If desired, eggplants may be left whole, slashed on one side, and some pulp removed with a small spoon. The pulp can then be sauteed with the filling and stuffed into the eggplant for an attractive method, especially for smaller eggplants. This variation can also be prepared on top of the stove.
